identificationModule.briefTitle: A Study to Evaluate Safety and Efficacy of TP-05 in Healthy Participants With Tick Exposure
identificationModule.officialTitle: A Phase 2b, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Efficacy of TP-05 in Healthy Participants at High Risk of Tick Exposure

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This study is designed to evaluate the safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetics of TP05 administered orally to healthy adult participants.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription: This is a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study conducted in healthy adult participants prior to anticipated exposure to Lyme Borreliosis. Participants will be randomized to receive either TP05 or placebo according to a predefined dosing schedule. Safety will be evaluated through adverse event monitoring, clinical laboratory assessments, vital signs, and physical examinations. The study will consist of a screening period, a treatment period (up to 24 weeks) and a safety follow up period. Participants will be randomized to receive one of two treatment regimens of TP-05 or placebo. Participants will be followed up for approximately 15 months and evaluated further for tick bites or symptoms of Lyme borreliosis.

interventions.name: TP-05 (lotilaner) Low Dose
interventions.description: TP05 administered orally at the protocol-defined preventative dose.

interventions.name: TP-05 (lotilaner) High Dose
interventions.description: TP05 administered orally at the protocol-defined preventative dose.

interventions.name: Placebo
interventions.description: Matching placebo administered orally according to the same dosing schedule as TP05.

primaryOutcomes.measure: The Incidence of Treatment Emergent Adverse Events From Baseline
primaryOutcomes.description: Safety and tolerability will be evaluated by incidence rate of treatment emergent adverse events from baseline.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From day 1 through the end of study follow-up, an average of 15 months.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Clinically Significant Changes From Baseline Chemistry Laboratory Tests
primaryOutcomes.description: Number of participants with clinically significant changes in clinical laboratory tests
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From day 1 through the end of study follow up, an average of 15 months.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Clinically Significant Changes From Baseline Hematology Laboratory Tests
primaryOutcomes.description: Number of participants with clinically significant changes in clinical laboratory tests.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From day 1 through the end of study follow up, an average of 15 months.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Clinically Significant Changes From Baseline Vital Signs
primaryOutcomes.description: Number of participants with clinically significant changes in vital signs.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From day 1 through the end of study follow up, an average of 15 months.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Clinically Significant Changes From Baseline Electrocardiograms (ECGs)
primaryOutcomes.description: Safety will be assessed by evaluating clinically significant changes from Baseline ECGs change in mean ventricular rate \[beats/min\].
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From day 1 through the end of study follow up, an average of 15 months.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Clinically Significant Changes From Baseline Electrocardiograms (ECGs) Measures
primaryOutcomes.description: Safety will be assessed by evaluating clinically significant changes from Baseline ECGs change in pulse rate \[msec\].
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From day 1 through the end of study follow up, an average of 15 months.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Clinically Significant Changes From Baseline QTC Interval
primaryOutcomes.description: Safety will be assessed by evaluating clinically significant changes from Baseline ECGs change in QTC interval
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From day 1 through the end of study follow up, an average of 15 months.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Clinically Significant Changes From Baseline QRS Interval
primaryOutcomes.description: Safety will be assessed by evaluating clinically significant changes from Baseline ECGs change in QRS interval.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From day 1 through the end of study follow up, an average of 15 months.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Concentration of Lotilaner in Whole Blood
secondaryOutcomes.description: Concentration of lotilaner in whole blood at specified timepoints measured using validated bioanalytical assays.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From dose through study completion, an average of 15 months.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Terminal Elimination Half Life (t½) of Lotilaner
secondaryOutcomes.description: Terminal elimination half life (t½) of lotilaner.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At protocol specified timepoints through end study treatment phase, an average of 28 weeks.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Area Under the Concentration Time Curve (AUC) of Lotilaner
secondaryOutcomes.description: Area under the concentration time curve (AUC) of lotilaner.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At protocol specified timepoints through end of pharmacokinetic sampling, an average of 15 months.
At protocol specified timepoints through end of pharmacokinetic sampling, an average of 15 months.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Time to Maximum Observed Concentration (Tmax) of Lotilaner
secondaryOutcomes.description: Time to maximum observed concentration (Tmax) of lotilaner.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: At protocol specified timepoints through end of pharmacokinetic sampling, an average of 15 months.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Overtly healthy adult participants aged 18 to 70 years
* Able to provide written informed consent
* Willing and able to comply with study procedures
* At high risk of exposure to ticks
* Contraceptive use by men and women consistent with local regulations

Exclusion Criteria:

* Prior exposure to TP05 or any isooxazoline in the last 12 months
* Known hypersensitivity to TP05 or related compounds
* Clinically significant medical conditions that may interfere with study participation
* Use of investigational products within 30 days prior to screening.
* Received previous vaccination against Lyme borreliosis, including investigational vaccines intended to prevent Lyme borreliosis
* Receiving long-term antibiotic therapy
* Received active or passive immunization within 4 weeks prior to Day
* Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals
